root_text.an8.72:1.1: “Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti, no ca sīlavā.
root_text.an8.72:1.5: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti sīlavā ca,
root_text.an8.72:2.1: Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti sīlavā ca, no ca bahussuto …pe…
root_text.an8.72:2.4: parisāvacaro ca, no ca visārado parisāya dhammaṁ deseti …pe…
root_text.an8.72:2.5: visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, no ca ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā te kāyena phusitvā viharati …pe…
root_text.an8.72:2.6: ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā te kāyena phusitvā viharati, no ca āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati;
root_text.an8.72:2.9: ‘kintāhaṁ saddho ca assaṁ, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseyyaṁ, ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā te kāyena phusitvā vihareyyaṁ, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an8.72:3.1: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti. Ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā te ca kāyena phusitvā viharati, āsavānañca khayā …pe… sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati;
root_text.an8.72:3.3: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu samantapāsādiko ca hoti sabbākāraparipūro cā”ti.

translation_text.an8.72:1.1: “A mendicant is faithful, but not ethical.
translation_text.an8.72:1.2: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an8.72:1.4: ‘How can I become faithful and ethical?’
translation_text.an8.72:1.5: When the mendicant is faithful and ethical,
translation_text.an8.72:2.1: A mendicant is faithful and ethical, but not learned. …
translation_text.an8.72:2.4: they don’t teach Dhamma to the assembly with assurance …
translation_text.an8.72:2.5: they don’t have direct meditative experience of the peaceful liberations that are formless, transcending form …
translation_text.an8.72:2.6: they don’t realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an8.72:2.7: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an8.72:2.9: ‘How can I become faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, one who gets the formless liberations, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements?’
translation_text.an8.72:3.1: When they’re faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, one who gets the formless liberations, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements,
translation_text.an8.72:3.3: A mendicant who possesses these eight qualities is impressive all around, and is complete in every respect.”
